title: Voice In, Visuals Out: The Agony and the Ecstasy - Allen Pike, Forestwalk Labs
summary: Allen Pike discusses voice-in, visuals-out as a superior AI UX paradigm, following Andrej Karpathy's thesis. The talk covers why this approach works for LLM-powered products, user delight factors, and techniques for achieving low latency in responsive AI agents.
writeup: Allen Pike of Forestwalk Labs argues that voice-in, visuals-out is a stronger UX paradigm for LLM-powered products, building on Andrej Karpathy's thesis. The talk explains why voice input paired with visual output suits these products, what drives user delight, and techniques for achieving low latency in responsive AI agents. The throughline: interface design, not just model capability, shapes whether AI products feel responsive and worth using.
